. Contingent strategies for Livestock, Poultry & Fisheries for District: BATHINDA .. Livestock Suggested contingency measures Before the event During the event After the event Drought Not applicable Floods Not applicable Cyclone Not applicable Cold wave Covering all the wire meshed walls / open area with gunny bags/ Allow for late grazing between 10AM to 3PM during cold Feed the animals as per routine polyethylene sheets (with mechanism for lifting during the day waves schedule time and putting down during night time) Add 25-50 ml of edible oil in concentrates and fed to the Allow the animals for grazing animal during cold waves (normal timings) In severe cases, put on the heaters at night times Apply / sprinkle lime powder in the animal shed during cold waves to neutralize ammonia accumulation Heat wave Arrangement for protection from heat wave Allow the animals early in the morning or late in the evening Feed the animals as per routine ) Plantation around the shed for grazing during heat waves schedule ii) HO sprinklers / foggers in the shed Feed green fodder (maize or perennial fodder)/silage / Allow the animals for grazing concentrates/complete feed or feed blocks during day time and (normal timings) iii) Application of white reflector paint on the roof roughages / hay during night time in case of heat waves iv) Thatched sheds should be provided as shelter to Put on the foggers / sprinkerlers/fans during heat weaves in animal to minimize heat stress case of high yielders (Jersey/HF crosses) In severe cases, vitamin ‘’ and electrolytes should be added in HO during heat waves. Insurance Encouraging insurance of livestock Listing out the details of the dead animals Submission for insurance claim and availing insurance benefit Purchase of new productive animals 20

In hot summer, add anti-stress probiotics in drinking water or feed 21
